metadata.title: Welfare Economics: Measuring Economic Well-Being
learning_objectives: Analyze welfare effects of price controls
Calculate CV and EV for exact welfare measurement
Compare approximate and exact welfare measures

questions.question: WTP per unit: 1st=$50, 2nd=$40, 3rd=$30, 4th=$20, 5th=$10. Price=$25. Find units purchased and CS.
hints: Consumer buys units where WTP ≥ price
CS = sum of (WTP - price) for each unit purchased

questions.question: Min price per unit: 1st=$8, 2nd=$12, 3rd=$16, 4th=$20, 5th=$24. Market price=$18. Find units supplied and PS.
Demand: Qᵈ = 7 - p, Supply: Qˢ = p - 1. Find equilibrium, CS, PS, and verify welfare maximum.
hints: CS = ½ × base × height (triangle under demand)
Test Q = 2 and Q = 4 to verify maximum

questions.question: Market: Qᵈ = 7 - p, Qˢ = p - 1. Price ceiling pᶜ = 2. Full welfare analysis.
Market: Qᵈ = 7 - p, Qˢ = p - 1. Price floor pᶠ = 5. Welfare analysis with government purchase.
Demand: Qᵈ = 10 - p, Supply: Qˢ = 4 (inelastic). Price ceiling pᶜ = 4. Show DWL = 0.

questions.topic: CV and EV Calculation - Price Decrease
questions.question: U = x₁x₂, m = 100, p₂ = 5. Price falls: p₁⁰ = 10 → p₁¹ = 5. Calculate CV, EV, ΔCS.
hints: For U = x₁x₂: demands are x₁ = m/(2p₁), x₂ = m/(2p₂)

questions.question: Same utility U = x₁x₂, m = 100, p₂ = 5. Price RISES: p₁⁰ = 5 → p₁¹ = 10. Calculate CV, EV, ΔCS.
State and apply the two fundamental welfare theorems.
Wheat: Qᵈ = 100 - 2p, Qˢ = 3p - 50. Price floor pᶠ = 35 with government purchase.
U = x^0.5 y^0.5, m = 200, pᵧ = 1. Price falls: pₓ = 4 → pₓ = 1. Compare CV, ΔCS, EV.
Write R functions for welfare calculations. Test with Pᵈ = 10 - Q, Pˢ = 2 + Q, ceiling pᶜ = 4.
